summary: Iteration Feb 6 2026 (PowerHour + Sponsor Achievement + Vibe-Drive Bearer auth + vote-skip). Backend pytest 71/71 PASSED. E2E smoke against public preview URL: 9/9 PASSED. Vote-skip 2-vote threshold verified end-to-end via Mongo direct insert (votes:2, skipped:true). Power Hour wiring into chair purchases confirmed in /app/backend/routes/chairs.py (lines 279-281, 353). FRONTEND: PowerHourBadge component is well-built but NOT mounted on any page yet — main agent must mount it (e.g. on /vault) for the data-testid='power-hour-badge' assertion to be verifiable in a live UI session.

integration_issues.issue: Component PowerHourBadge.tsx exists with correct data-testid='power-hour-badge', polling, and active/inactive variants — but is never imported/mounted on any page (verified via grep across /app/frontend/src). The review-request explicitly asks to lazy-mount it on /vault (or similar) and verify the testid in DOM. Without a mount, the badge cannot render in production.

action_items: Lazy-mount <PowerHourBadge /> onto /vault (or any chair-purchase landing surface) so data-testid='power-hour-badge' appears in production DOM. Component is import-ready at /app/frontend/src/components/chairs/PowerHourBadge.tsx. Use React.lazy/Suspense per the review request wording.
Optional: also surface PowerHourBadge on the chair-purchase modal/page so the 1.10× nudge is visible at the exact moment users buy.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/power_hour_sponsors.py — clean, well-documented, server-authoritative time check (good). _maybe_grant_free_chair correctly uses idem_key for grants and chair_purchases mirror; lifetime cap of 3 enforced via min(earned, MAX). Loop logic at line 222-258 catches duplicate idem_key insertions safely.

routes/vibe_drive.py vote-skip — threshold computed as voters//2+1 (simple majority). Default voters=2 when no rides row → threshold=2. Idempotent upsert by (ride_id, user_id, track_uri). Audit trail in vibe_drive_skip_votes + vibe_drive_skip_history. Verified working end-to-end.
routes/chairs.py wiring — is_power_hour_active() called at purchase time; weight multiplied by POWER_HOUR_MULT (1.10); power_hour_bonus stamped on row. Spec-compliant.
PowerHourBadge.tsx — silent on fetch errors (good UX), polls every 30s, returns null if status not yet fetched. Only gap: not mounted anywhere yet.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iter_feb6_2026_power_hour_sponsors_e2e.py (NEW — public-URL E2E smoke for P0/P1/P2 deliverables)

success_rate.frontend: N/A — badge not mounted; cannot live-verify rendering

test_credentials: POST /api/auth/demo-login (no body) — returns {token, user_id}; auth_token Bearer header works on all protected routes
seed_data_creation: Created/cleaned ephemeral test data: vibe_sponsors (TEST_QA Pizza), vibe_drive_skip_votes (qa_ride_*); used ambassador_id=qa_amb_<hex> in test_sponsor_link_then_admin_verify_grants_free_chair (with full teardown).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Power Hour, Sponsor Achievement, Vibe-Drive Bearer auth, and vote-skip backend deliverables are verified GREEN against the public URL. The only remaining gap is the PowerHourBadge component not being lazy-mounted on a page (e.g. /vault). After main agent mounts it, run a quick playwright check: navigate to /vault (after demo-login) and assert page.query_selector('[data-testid="power-hour-badge"]') is not None. The badge has data-active='true' or 'false' attribute reflecting backend status.
rca of the issue: PowerHourBadge.tsx was authored as a standalone component but never imported into any page/layout. grep across /app/frontend/src/**/*.{js,jsx,ts,tsx} shows zero usages outside the file itself. Mitigation: add `const PowerHourBadge = lazy(() => import('@/components/chairs/PowerHourBadge'))` to the target page, wrap in <Suspense fallback={null}>, and place near the chair-purchase CTA.
